summary: Verified mobile (412x915) result card fits within viewport with no right-edge clipping and no horizontal page scroll after the min-w-0 fix on the reveal-stage column.
seed_data_creation: None. Ran one live shuffle (1 billed Google Places call) as instructed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fix confirmed. Mobile 412x915 metrics: innerWidth=412, clientWidth=412, documentElement.scrollWidth=412, body.scrollWidth=412 (NO horizontal overflow). Result card rect left=41 right=371 width=330 — fully inside viewport. All 7 action buttons (doordash-button, order-online-button, rate-on-google-button, respin-button, share-fate-button, share-fate-image-button, reset-spin-button) present, none clipped; they wrap onto multiple rows (share-fate-image-button and reset-spin-button share row at top=487.75, others stacked). Restaurant returned: 'At Nine Restaurant & Bar', 4.5, 0.5 mi away, 592 9th Ave NY. Screenshot shows card fully rendered within viewport width.
rca of the issue: Original bug: right-hand reveal-stage grid column lacked min-w-0, so the CSS grid track expanded to intrinsic content width, pushing the result card past the viewport edge on narrow screens. Fix (adding min-w-0 to the right column div in Home.jsx line ~728) allows the grid track to shrink and constrains the card to the available column width. Verified via document.documentElement.scrollWidth === window.innerWidth (412 === 412) and card bounding rect right (371) < innerWidth (412).
